# README

3rdPartyBaseLibs is a meta-project that contains the base libraries used in most projects

<!-- BEGIN AUTOGENERATED CODE - DO NOT EDIT BELOW THIS POINT - IT WILL BE OVERWRITTEN -->

## How To Setup Secrects in Github to allow actions to run

The metaproject uses a standard named `secret` to allow access to repository

In order to setup it, follow the next steps:

### Generate a new secret

- go to https://github.com/settings/tokens or navigate to it via `user icon (on top right)` -> `settings` -> `<> Developer Settings` -> `Personal Access Tokens` -> `Tokens (classic)`
- `Generate new token` -> `Generate new token (classic)`
- Add a `Note` (e.g. you meta project name)
- Select a Expiration
- Add `repo` checkbox
- `Generate token` (page bottom)

Make sure to copy the token (usually it starts with `ghp_...`)

### Add the token to the meta-project

- Go to the repository of this meta-project
- `Settings` -> `Secrects and variables` -> `Actions`
- In `Repository secrets` create a `New Repository Settings`
- Name it `ACTIONS_ORG_ACCESS` and paste the token created at the step above

Manually re-run the workflows or wait for a auto triggered build
